channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline) - Space
item.description:
1. NASA Artemis 2 astronauts to make historic moon flyby today. Here's what to expect hour by hour (timeline)  Space

  4. Don't miss these breathtaking photos of Artemis II NASA shared  USA Today
  5. Artemis crew reaches the moon, approaches record-breaking distance from Earth  reuters.com

item.description:
1. 'This generation's moment': How the Artemis missions will reframe humanity's relationship with the moon  Live Science

item.title: Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13’s record - AL.com
item.description:
1. Artemis II toilet acts up again as astronauts speed toward the moon to break Apollo 13’s record  AL.com

  4. On NASA’s Moon Mission, the Biggest Headache Has Been the Toilet  Bloomberg.com
  5. Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine  Space

item.title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more' - Space
item.description:

item.title: Newly discovered fossils give scientists first look at the evolution of early complex animals - PBS
item.description:

item.title: Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Exploding primordial black holes might have reshaped the early universe, and created all matter as we know it  Phys.org
  2. Did Scientists Just Detect an Exploding Black Hole?  The New York Times
  3. LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial Black Hole, Scientists Say  ScienceAlert
  4. Rare Gravitational Wave Discovery May Hold the Key to First Evidence of Primordial Black Holes  The Daily Galaxy
  5. Is the universe swarming with tiny black holes?  Yahoo
